description: Add one or more tags to an existing task

changes: Initial implementation for task tags feature

task_id.description: ID of the task to add tags to. Must be owned by the current user.

tags.description: Array of tags to add. Tags will be normalized to lowercase and deduplicated. Maximum 20 tags total per task.

task.description: Updated task object with merged tags

tags.description: All tags on the task (existing + newly added, deduplicated)

tags_added.description: List of tags that were actually added (excludes duplicates)

natural_language_examples.intent: Add 'work' tag to task with ID 5
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: Tag 'work' is added to task 5. If task already has 'work', no duplicate is created.

natural_language_examples.user_input: add tags urgent and high-priority to task 3
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: Both tags are added to task 3, normalized to lowercase

natural_language_examples.user_input: tag task 1 with shopping, groceries, and personal
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: All 3 tags are added to task 1

natural_language_examples.user_input: add Work and URGENT tags to task 7
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: Tags are normalized to 'work' and 'urgent' before adding

error_handling.trigger: Task ID does not exist or not owned by user

response.message: Task with ID {task_id} not found or not accessible
Task cannot have more than 20 tags total. Currently has {current_count}, trying to add {add_count}.

error_handling.trigger: Tag contains invalid characters or too long

response.message: Tag '{tag}' is invalid. Tags must be 1-50 characters, alphanumeric with spaces, hyphens, underscores only.

performance_requirements.implementation_approach: Direct JSONB array merge in PostgreSQL

security_requirements: User isolation: Can only add tags to own tasks

understanding_commands: User says 'add tag X to task N' → add_tag(task_id=N, tags=['X'])
User says 'tag task N with X, Y, Z' → add_tag(task_id=N, tags=['X', 'Y', 'Z'])
User says 'add urgent tag to buy groceries' → First find task by title, then add tag
Multiple tags separated by 'and', ',', 'or' → Parse into array
response_formatting: Confirm which tags were added: 'Added tags 'work', 'urgent' to task "{title}"'
If duplicate tags skipped: 'Task already has tags: {skipped_tags}'
Show all current tags: 'Task now has tags: {all_tags}'

operations: SELECT task with user_id and task_id (ownership check)
UPDATE task SET tags = tags || new_tags (array merge)

expected_output.note: No duplicates added, task still has 1 'work' tag
